title: gladiolus communis subsp. byzantinus
intro: Gladiolus communis subsp. byzantinus, also known as the Byzantine gladiolus or autumn crocus, is a plant species belonging to the family Iridaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been shown to help treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Gladiolus communis subsp. byzantinus include its potential to help lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of certain diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
